top_articles.reason: Tragic incident with fatalities and injuries at a major airport.
top_articles.title: NTSB Team Still En Route To LaGuardia: Delayed By TSA Lines
top_articles.summary_en: The NTSB has begun its investigation into the fatal collision of Air Canada flight AC8646 with a firetruck at LaGuardia Airport, confirming two fatalities among the pilots. While the agency has recovered the black boxes, delays in the investigation are occurring due to long TSA lines affecting the arrival of specialists.
top_articles.summary_zh: 美国国家运输安全委员会（NTSB）已开始对在拉瓜迪亚机场发生的加拿大航空航班AC8646与消防车相撞的致命事件进行调查，确认机长中有两人遇难。尽管该机构已恢复黑匣子，但由于安检线长，专家的到达受到影响，导致调查进展缓慢。

top_articles.reason: Significant regulatory approval impacting aircraft operational capabilities.
top_articles.title: Boeing 787 Range Boost: FAA Approves 400 Extra Miles For The Dreamliner
top_articles.summary_en: The FAA has approved an increase in the maximum takeoff weight for the Boeing 787 Dreamliner, allowing the 787-9 and 787-10 models to fly an additional 400 miles or carry more payload. This upgrade, driven by airline feedback, enhances the aircraft's capabilities for long-haul routes and supports airlines like Air New Zealand in expanding their network.
top_articles.summary_zh: 美国联邦航空局已批准波音787梦想飞机的最大起飞重量增加，使787-9和787-10型号能够飞行额外的400英里或承载更多货物。此升级是基于航空公司反馈，增强了飞机在长途航线上的能力，并支持像新西兰航空这样的航空公司扩展其网络。

top_articles.reason: Military aircraft crash with a high number of casualties.
top_articles.title: 110 Soldiers Onboard: Lockheed C-130 Crashes
top_articles.summary_en: A Lockheed C-130 Hercules military transport aircraft crashed near Puerto Leguízamo, Colombia, on March 23, 2026, shortly after takeoff, with 125 people on board, including 110 soldiers. While 77 survivors have been rescued, around 40 individuals remain unaccounted for as search efforts continue, prompting investigations into the causes of the crash and reviews of similar aircraft.
top_articles.summary_zh: 2026年3月23日，一架洛克希德C-130大力士军用运输机在哥伦比亚的普埃尔托·莱吉萨莫附近坠毁，起飞不久后机上有125人，包括110名士兵。虽然已有77名幸存者被救出，但仍有约40人失踪，搜救工作正在进行中，这促使对坠毁原因的调查和对类似飞机的审查。

top_articles.reason: Retirement of a popular aircraft model affecting airline operations.
top_articles.title: Why Airlines Are Retiring the 757
top_articles.summary_en: The Boeing 757, once a versatile and powerful aircraft beloved by airlines for its ability to operate on short runways and handle various routes, is being retired as newer models like the Airbus A321XLR take its place in the long-haul narrowbody market. This shift reflects changing airline needs and advancements in aircraft technology.
top_articles.summary_zh: 波音757曾是航空公司喜爱的多功能强大飞机，因其能够在短跑道上起降并处理各种航线而备受青睐，但随着空客A321XLR等新机型的出现，757正在被淘汰。这一变化反映了航空公司需求的变化和飞机技术的进步。

top_articles.reason: Introduction of new ultra-long routes by a major airline.
top_articles.title: Up To 17-Hour Nonstop Flights: Cathay Pacific's 10 New Ultra-Long Routes In 2026
top_articles.summary_en: Cathay Pacific is set to launch 10 new ultra-long routes in 2026, with daily flights from Hong Kong averaging 174, a 9% increase from the previous year. The longest route will be from Dallas/Fort Worth to Hong Kong, with a maximum block time of 17 hours and 15 minutes, influenced by airspace restrictions over Russia.
top_articles.summary_zh: 国泰航空将在2026年推出10条新的超长航线，从香港出发的每日航班平均达到174班，比去年增长9%。最长的航线将是从达拉斯/沃斯堡到香港，最大航程时间为17小时15分钟，受俄罗斯空域限制的影响。

shownotes_en:
Welcome back to the Aviation Pulse podcast! I’m your host, Dudu, and today is March 24, 2026. We have some intriguing stories lined up for you, so let’s dive right in.

First up, we’re following the investigation into the tragic collision at LaGuardia Airport involving Air Canada flight AC8646 and a firetruck. The National Transportation Safety Board, or NTSB, has confirmed that two pilots lost their lives in this accident. While the agency has successfully retrieved the black boxes, the investigation is facing some delays. It turns out that long TSA lines are hampering the arrival of their specialists. It’s a stark reminder of how logistics can impact critical safety investigations.

Now, shifting gears to some exciting news from Boeing. The FAA has just approved an increase in the maximum takeoff weight for the Boeing 787 Dreamliner, allowing the 787-9 and 787-10 models to fly an additional 400 miles or carry more payload. This is a significant upgrade that many airlines, like Air New Zealand, have been eagerly anticipating. It enhances the Dreamliner’s capabilities for long-haul routes, making it even more attractive for airlines looking to expand their networks.

Speaking of expansions and challenges, we have some sobering news from Colombia. A Lockheed C-130 Hercules military transport aircraft tragically crashed shortly after takeoff near Puerto Leguízamo on March 23. The aircraft was carrying 125 people, including 110 soldiers. While 77 survivors have been rescued, around 40 individuals remain unaccounted for, and search efforts are ongoing. This incident is prompting investigations into the causes of the crash and reviews of similar aircraft operations.

On a different note, let’s talk about the Boeing 757. Once a favorite among airlines for its versatility, the 757 is now being retired as newer models, like the Airbus A321XLR, take the spotlight in the long-haul narrowbody market. This shift reflects not only changing airline needs but also advancements in aircraft technology that allow for more efficient operations. It’s fascinating to see how the aviation landscape evolves over time.

And finally, we have some exciting developments from Cathay Pacific. The airline is set to launch 10 new ultra-long routes in 2026, with daily flights from Hong Kong increasing by 9% compared to last year. The longest of these routes will connect Dallas/Fort Worth to Hong Kong, with a maximum block time of 17 hours and 15 minutes. This expansion is influenced by airspace restrictions over Russia, showcasing how geopolitical factors can affect flight planning.

That wraps up our top stories for today. Thank you for joining me on this journey through the skies. I’m Dudu, and I look forward to bringing you more aviation news in our next episode. Until then, safe travels and clear skies!
shownotes_zh:
欢迎回到《航空脉动》播客！我是你的主持人Dudu，今天是2026年3月24日。我们有一些引人入胜的故事要分享，所以让我们直接开始吧。

首先，我们关注发生在拉瓜迪亚机场的悲惨事故，涉及加拿大航空的AC8646航班与一辆消防车相撞。国家运输安全委员会（NTSB）确认此次事故导致两名飞行员遇难。虽然该机构已成功恢复黑匣子，但调查却因TSA安检排队时间过长而面临一些延误。这提醒我们，物流如何影响关键的安全调查。

接下来，我们来看看波音的好消息。FAA刚刚批准了对波音787梦想飞机最大起飞重量的提高，使787-9和787-10型号可以多飞400英里或运载更多货物。这是许多航空公司（如新西兰航空）期待已久的重要升级。它增强了梦想飞机在长途航线上的能力，使其对希望扩展网络的航空公司更具吸引力。

说到扩张与挑战，我们来看看哥伦比亚的一个悲惨事件。一架洛克希德C-130大力士军用运输机于3月23日在靠近普埃尔托·莱吉萨莫起飞后不久坠毁。机上共有125人，包括110名士兵。虽然已有77名幸存者被救出，但约40人仍下落不明，搜救工作仍在进行中。这起事件引发了对坠毁原因的调查以及对类似飞机操作的审查。

另一方面，让我们聊聊波音757。这款曾经广受航空公司喜爱的多用途飞机，如今正被逐步退役，因为更新型号，如空客A321XLR，正在长途窄体市场中崭露头角。这一转变不仅反映了航空公司的需求变化，也体现了飞机技术的进步，使得运营更加高效。看到航空行业的演变真是令人着迷。

最后，我们有来自国泰航空的激动人心的消息。该航空公司计划于2026年推出10条新的超长航线，从香港出发的每日航班数量较去年增加了9%。其中最长的航线将连接达拉斯/沃斯堡和香港，最大航程时间为17小时15分钟。这一扩展受到俄罗斯上空限制的影响，展示了地缘政治因素如何影响航班规划。

今天的头条故事就到这里。感谢您与我一同踏上这段天空之旅。我是Dudu，期待在下期节目中为您带来更多航空新闻。在此之前，祝您安全旅行，晴空万里！
